name: Shrimps with Basil, Leeks and Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Lemon juice or white wine
recipeInstructions:
"With the sauce prepared in advance, this colorful dish makes a quick entree for two, good served over fluffy rice."
1. Marinate the prepared shrimp several hours in lemon juice or white wine, with half the garlic and half the basil.
2. Cut leeks lengthwise and wash well under running cold water, then chop them.
3. Heat 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet until hot. Add leeks and sauté for 5 minutes, or until soft. Add remaining garlic and tomatoes; cook over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes. Add the remaining basil and salt and pepper to taste. Cook until thick.
4. Refrigerate the mixture overnight if desired.
5. Several minutes before serving, reheat the leek-tomato mixture in the skillet over medium-high heat.
6. In a separate pan, heat the remaining butter and oil until sizzling. Add shrimp and cook on one side for 40 seconds, or until they turn pink. Turn and cook on the remaining side for another 40 seconds.
7. Pour the heated leek-tomato mixture over the shrimp and cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
8. Serve immediately over rice pilaf, garnished with whole basil sprigs.
Yield: Serves 2 as an entree, or 3 as an appetizer.
